pattern: Manhattan distance splits into two independent +1/-1 walks along the two diagonals, and since a change lets you replace any step with whichever direction you like, greedily 'spending' a change on the very next unhelpful step (as soon as you still have budget) maximizes every running total simultaneously.
hints: |x| + |y| equals the largest of four combinations: (x+y), (x-y), (-x+y), (-x-y). Each combination turns into a running +1/-1 tally over the string if you assign +1 to two of the four directions and -1 to the other two.

For one such tally, you're allowed to change up to k characters to whichever direction is best; the best move for maximizing every running prefix total is to flip the very first k steps that would otherwise contribute -1, turning them into +1 as soon as you encounter them and still have budget left.
Run this greedy left-to-right scan for all four +1/-1 assignments and take the largest running maximum seen across all four; that is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: zero changes allowed, just the raw walk
k covers the whole string, every step can be chosen freely
the walk already cancels itself out without any changes
changes are best spent early, not on the single worst step overall

stress.description: random walks over N/S/E/W with a random change budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100000)))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('NSEW') for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(0, n)
    return [s, k]
